title: I'm a tech founder, so I built an autonomous AI agent that trades crypto 24/7 on Solana. Here's what happened after 30 days of real trading
author: ok-hacker
selftext:
I've been curious whether current AI models have any natural aptitude for trading on realtime, raw financial data, without any elaborate news pipelines or convoluted system prompts. I mean literally just raw livestreamed market numbers and a strategy layer.

So I built andmilo, an autonomous trading agent on Solana. The agent consumes realtime market data and manages a portfolio 24/7. It has access to multiple strategies (trend following, mean reversion, breakout detection) and autonomously decides when to enter or exit positions based on risk parameters and market conditions.  


https://preview.redd.it/ebej6jb8r0ng1.png?width=2774&format=png&auto=webp&s=1d761939723a7e7a986ca9f712dc3aa54546786c

Example thesis from Meme Sniper Strategy's arena wallet:

https://preview.redd.it/fx9iskajr0ng1.png?width=1380&format=png&auto=webp&s=5568599d1142e461635d0a5928d5edad9aaf8e22

Example strategy settings:  


https://preview.redd.it/4knbw3kas0ng1.png?width=2028&format=png&auto=webp&s=31ead471b4821240b2d784c13b10d27e8d4c1145

  
  
I started with a modest account and let it run completely autonomously for 30 days. The agent analyzes market structure, manages position sizing, and adapts to different volatility regimes - all without manual intervention.

What's interesting is seeing how the AI handles different market conditions. During choppy sideways markets, it stays mostly flat. When clear trends emerge, it scales into positions. The risk management layer prevents it from over-leveraging even when the model gets "confident."

This isn't a simulated arena - it's real money, real execution, real slippage. The agent is trading against other humans and bots in the actual Solana ecosystem.

You can check it out at [andmilo.com](http://andmilo.com) (referral code: karsus if you want to try it)

comments.author: Puzzled_Web5062
comments.body: “Clear trends” it trades when it goes up a lot or down a lot? Lol

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
Nope, strategy embedded with a dsl language toto filter opportunities as they come by. LLM choose strategy according to detected regime.

Then, if it passed the playbook gates, it receives different resolution candles and the instructions in order to decide if to trade, and with what levels

replies.author: whatthewhat69
replies.body: I'm sure it will work out and you'll be able to beat the ML algos that wall street has been working on for 30 years.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: Not trying to beat Wall Street's quant algos on their own turf. Different market, different edge - Solana meme coins have thin liquidity and no institutional players running HFT against you. The inefficiencies are real, they're just different.

replies.author: Potential-Recipe4734
replies.body: I’m building the exact same intelligence system haha it’s going great  paper trading so far I avg 49% win rate with my dynamic positioning and regime state adjuster, grok ai, trade sentinel, multiple safety gates, Reentry engine, tight exits.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: 49% on paper trading is solid, nice stack too - multiple safety gates and reentry logic is exactly the right approach. The jump from paper to live is real though, execution latency and slippage will hit differently. Curious how your regime state adjuster handles transitions - that's usually where the edge cases appear.

comments.author: FrostySquirrel820
comments.body: You forgot to include the paragraph where you tell us what happened after 30 days of trading.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: Oops! Gonna update it (but it’s in the image)

comments.author: Hopeful_Pressure
comments.body: Negative PnL on all three strategies.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
there are more than 3. people spin up more strategies and throw them into the arena.

I can't seem to be able to add images in the comments though.  
Check [app.andmilo.com/arena](http://app.andmilo.com/arena) to see the leaderboard

comments.author: orange-cola
comments.body: Some language in this post seems familiar, just can't put my finger on it

comments.author: Playful-Variety-1242
comments.body: Ok I just gave Claude access to my bank accounts. I’ll probably put my two week notice in tomorrow🤑

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body:
If you ask an LLM what it would do to make money, if it had full control of your computer - it’s always some social media things and it’s gonna get rich by trading equities and crypto.  

Then you ask it to define the crypto strategies. Still very vague but sounds great. 

Then you ask it to start writing scripts and it does the same stuff.  Level 2 order book , volume, bla bla.  

Because it knows what we know. 

Of course crypto trading is extremely different than the stock market. An idiot can 3-5x during a bull market if they are positioned before it starts. 

Trying to trading during the bull rises usually yields less than riding to the top. 

Then the down trend is extremely misleading, squeezing the last dollars from retail.  Institutions have already sold. 

And this horizontal crap we have been in, it’s better to go stocks than crypto. Which is why billions left crypto months ago. 

Ai absolutely can not predict the future. And that only leaves math. Involving an LLM just adds latency.  It’s a joke.  Yes, idiots will go g for any get rich quick scheme. 

Meanwhile I loaded up on MU the day when they announced they were shutting down consumer mfg to focus on ai mfg - causing the insane memory shortage.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: Different approach - milo uses a separate wallet you control, not your bank account. Though your notice-putting-in timeline sounds ambitious.

comments.author: Bitter_Broccoli_7536
comments.body: Honestly, the most impressive part to me is the risk management actually working autonomously. So many bots just blow up the first time volatility spikes. The fact that it stayed flat in choppy markets is a huge green flag

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
I had to put in many safeguards that keeps ai from itself.

But frankly, the newest models are out of this world and I wish I already had shorts implemented

comments.author: Extreme_Turnover7113
comments.body:
Running something similar but with equities instead of crypto. 30 days in on a momentum breakout strategy — volume confirmation turned out to be the real filter, not the price signal itself.

Curious what your win rate looks like broken down by regime detection accuracy. That's where ours leaks most.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
Interesting - volume confirmation as the real filter makes a lot of sense. I've seen the same on the meme coin side, price signal alone is too noisy, volume and wallet concentration end up being the real signal.



On regime detection: my win rate in trending markets is much higher than in chop, and that's the main leak too. I'm currently working on making the regime classifier more conservative - essentially having it require stronger confirmation before calling a trend, to cut down on false positives in messy conditions. Curious what you're using for your regime classifier.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
You can see it in the image. 
Best strategy so far with about 5%.

Which is quite good for the volatile crypto market.

But I think that now that the agentic trading is in place, it’s just a matter of us finding the best strategies

replies.author: zesty1989
replies.body:
Do you have a minimum balance?

How do you make money through my use of the AI?

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: Trading requires a minimum deposit of 1 SOL to activate. Without it, the bot cannot trade. I currently charge a 0.9% transaction fee and am now introducing paid tiers with model selection, along with subscription pricing

comments.author: Classic_Chemical_237
comments.body: If your platform makes money, why would you make money by getting users for your platform? Isn’t it more sensible to put all your money into your platform and go enjoying your life?

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body:
I didn't find the strategy that prints.  
In general, the platform work in such a way that the milo general agents learns from all the participants and adjust it's gates, etc accordingly

comments.author: Original_Spring_2808
comments.body: Love seeing agents handle volatility. I learned with crypto renegades training is that even automated systems benefit from clear trade frameworks and risk rules it makes a huge difference in choppy market.

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: 100% - the framework matters more than the model. The best-performing agents I've seen all have rigid rules about when NOT to trade, not just when to. Risk rules and regime filters are what keep it alive through volatility, the alpha is almost secondary.

comments.author: Dazzling_Wolverine43
comments.body: Software engineer here, what API's do you use for trading in real time and how do you adjust to be in real time?

replies.author: ok-hacker
replies.body: Jupiter and Raydium for execution on Solana, Helius for RPC and transaction data, Birdeye for token analytics. For real-time readiness we run a continuous loop rather than event-driven -- the agent evaluates market state every few minutes and pre-computes potential trades so when conditions hit it can execute in under a second. The bottleneck is almost always RPC congestion, not our code.
